_comment: Regression budgets for the chat message searchbench (#13534). run-all.mjs / searchbench-kpi.ts compare MEASURED aggregates against these and exit non-zero when a hard budget regresses. A metric is only checked when it was really measured against a >=10k-row PGlite corpus; an unmeasured (null) metric fails closed, never passes. Quality budgets guard the correctness fixes (multi-word non-adjacent recall, older-than-window recall, phrase precision). The latency budget is capability-aware: with pg_trgm the substring/partial fallback is a trigram-GIN index scan (maxP95LatencyMsTrigram, cloud/production); without it the fallback is an unindexed scan of the STORED message_search_document column (maxP95LatencyMsNoTrigram, the bundled PGlite build, ~10x slower under WASM). Both still catch the pre-materialization O(n)-recompute regression (which was ~11s). Ratchet the ceilings down as the plan tightens; raise the quality floors only when the gold set proves it.
